title: sutera hispida
intro: Sutera hispida, also known as the hairy zinnia or Mexican gold poppy, is a plant species belonging to the family Polemoniaceae. It is a flowering plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, known for its vibrant yellow flowers and soft, hairy leaves. Sutera hispida has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Sutera hispida can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
